topic: event-stream.ts to core http sse port ROI
trigger: user 2026-04-27 prompt: fire kick(ω-cycle) on 'event-stream.ts to core http sse port ROI' with axes design-strategy + falsifier-coverage and land witness JSON at the declared path.
refresh_mode: overwrite-in-place after live-tree revalidation; prior negative-ROI verdict preserved because the cited topology, completion, and alternative-fit evidence still holds in the current tree.
rule: raw 47 (cross-repo trawl-witness, design-strategy fan-out) + raw 48 (K>=3 orthogonal axes correlation < 0.7) + raw 73 (proof-carrying honesty admissibility) + raw 80 (sentinel format)

gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_oeis invocation=tool/oeis_live_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)

kernel_steps.1_intake_topic: Evaluate whether EventStream<T,R> from hive/packages/ai/src/utils/event-stream.ts should be ported into the coding-agent core HTTP SSE transport.
kernel_steps.2_pre_register_falsifiers: F100..F105 fixed before judging any candidate unification path.
kernel_steps.3_fan_out_axes: K=5 orthogonal axes: topology, completion semantics, blast radius, payload shape, alternatives/test surface.
kernel_steps.4_gate_tier1: Promote only if the falsifier pattern supports a stable design conclusion.

source_primitive.shape: generic EventStream<T,R> implementing AsyncIterable<T> with queue[], waiting[], done flag, finalResultPromise, and AssistantMessageEventStream specialization
source_primitive.topology: single producer, effectively single consumer queue semantics because push() resolves waiting.shift() and iteration drains one shared queue
source_primitive.completion_semantics: terminal completion driven by event.type==='done' or 'error'; result() exposes Promise<AssistantMessage>

target_port.shape: Node http SSE adapter over server bus
target_port.topology: single in-process bus publisher to N concurrent SSE clients, each with its own subscribeAll handler and optional sessionID filter
target_port.completion_semantics: connection stays open until disconnect; heartbeat frames keep the stream alive

existing_alternative.shape: typed EventEmitter bus with publish(), subscribe(), subscribeAll(), listenerCount(), clear()
existing_alternative.fit: already satisfies SSE multi-consumer fan-out directly

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: topology-match
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: EventStream can replace or directly underwrite SSE fan-out without per-client duplication or dropped events.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: terminal-mismatch
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: EventStream terminal completion contract aligns with open-ended SSE lifetime.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: blast-radius-bounded
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: Meaningful port/unification stays under roughly 100 LOC or a handful of call sites.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: payload-shape-overlap
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: AssistantMessageEvent and ServerEvent share enough structure to amortize a common primitive.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: alternative-already-fits
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: No current primitive already cleanly solves the SSE requirement.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: test-surface-stable
pre_registered_falsifiers.predicate: Porting/unifying does not destabilize the provider-tier result() contract.

axes_implemented.decision: Port is refuted by topology. EventStream.push() wakes only one waiter via waiting.shift(), and async iteration drains one shared queue; SSE needs every connected client to observe every bus event.

Port is refuted by completion semantics. EventStream is built around a one-shot finalResultPromise and auto-completion on done/error, while SSE is intentionally long-lived and heartbeat-driven until disconnect.

A narrow port inside sse.ts is small but useless; a broad unification is expensive. Current tree shows 13 provider files directly importing or threading AssistantMessageEventStream, plus ai/src/types.ts, ai/src/api-registry.ts, ai/src/stream.ts, coding-agent/src/core/model-registry.ts, and coding-agent/src/core/extensions/types.ts.
Port is refuted by payload-shape disjointness. AssistantMessageEvent is an LLM token/tool delta protocol; ServerEvent is a session and tool lifecycle observability protocol with 19 topic strings.

bus.subscribeAll already fits SSE, and ai/src/stream.ts still relies on s.result() for complete()/completeSimple(). A shared primitive would either break that contract or reintroduce the current split under a different name.

tier_1_promotions.proposition: Do not port event-stream.ts into the core HTTP SSE adapter. Keep EventStream as the single-consumer terminal-completion provider primitive, and keep bus.ts plus sse.ts as the multi-consumer open-ended transport primitive.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: F100=PASS, F101=PASS, F103=PASS, F104=PASS, F105=PASS; F102 only passes under the narrow interpretation that adds no value.

tier_1_promotions.proposition: If a future non-HTTP consumer truly needs a for-await shape over bus events, add a small busToAsyncIterable(filter?) adapter near bus.ts instead of porting EventStream.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: preserves multi-consumer topology, avoids finalResult coupling, and keeps provider-tier contracts intact
tier_1_promotions.promotion_gate: conditional on a real caller emerging

roi_verdict.summary: The port loses on topology, completion semantics, payload overlap, and alternatives. Narrow-scope reuse adds abstraction with no payoff; broad-scope unification expands churn into the provider stack without solving an actual problem in SSE.
roi_verdict.alternative_recommendation: status quo now; tiny bus-to-AsyncIterable adapter later only if a concrete caller appears

F100_topology_match.evidence: event-stream.ts push() resolves waiting.shift() and iterator drains one shared queue; sse.ts installs one subscribeAll consumer per client.

F101_terminal_mismatch.evidence: EventStream exposes finalResultPromise/result(); SSE stays open with heartbeat until disconnect.

F102_blast_radius_bounded.evidence: Broad unification reaches 13 provider files plus shared API/type surfaces; narrow reuse only touches sse.ts but provides no new capability.

F103_payload_shape_overlap.evidence: ServerEventTopic enumerates 19 lifecycle topics; AssistantMessageEvent is a different protocol family.

F104_alternative_already_fits.evidence: server/bus.ts already provides subscribeAll(), the exact fan-out primitive consumed by sse.ts.

F105_test_surface_stable.evidence: ai/src/stream.ts complete() and completeSimple() still call s.result(); provider implementations still construct AssistantMessageEventStream.

honesty_triad.write_barrier: no source mutations outside this witness artifact
no_fabrication_evidence: Read /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/ai/src/utils/event-stream.ts end-to-end; verified queue[], waiting[], done flag, result(), and AssistantMessageEventStream specialization.
Read /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/coding-agent/src/server/sse.ts end-to-end; verified subscribeAll fan-out, heartbeat timer, session filter, and disconnect cleanup.
Read /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/coding-agent/src/server/bus.ts end-to-end; verified typed publish/subscribe/subscribeAll primitive already exists.
Read /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/ai/src/stream.ts; verified complete() and completeSimple() depend on s.result().
Re-counted direct provider imports/usages of AssistantMessageEventStream in /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/ai/src/providers: 13 files.

fixpoint_marker: REACHED - T1a is stable now; T1b remains a future option only if a real AsyncIterable consumer appears.

next_cycle_handoff.purpose: only reopen if a real bus consumer needs AsyncIterable shape

items.task: If a new consumer wants for-await over bus events, implement a local adapter instead of reusing EventStream.
Do not refactor these primitives absent a new requirement.
